full_text:
Instructions for chemical inventory

General information

URL of inventory https://kemikaali.kemia.helsinki.fi , browser to be used is Internet Explorer

Virtual desktop interface for MAC computers/home use https://vdi.helsinki.fi

Flamma page of inventory (in Finnish only) with browsing instructions (in English)

https://flamma.helsinki.fi/portal/units/matlu?_nfpb=true&_pageLabel=P9806821561360920572974&contentId=HY334334&placeId=HY290370

Contact information for Erja (technical support for inventory but you can of course be in contact with superusers (contact informations in link above)):

02941 59479, erja.piitulainen@helsinki.fi

Creating a location

Go to location where you want to create a new location (Browse – choose location from tree structure).

Choose from upper left corner Location > New.

Every field marked in red needs to be filled. Ensure first that in field Parent Location is the location under which you want to create a new location. If not, please choose the correct location using Browse.

In Barcode Description ”Autogenerate” is chosen by default. You can leave it is as is unless you want to choose the barcode yourself.

In Location Name you would give a name for location.

In Location Type you can define the type of location. Usually ”unknown” or ”laboratory” is fine but you can choose for example Freezer or Refridgerator from menu. Icon of the location will then change into freezer/refridgerator.

In Description you can add a description of location if needed.

In Location may contain please choose Containers, plates and/or locations, if you want to add both subfolders and containers into the location to be created (that is the situation in most cases). If you are creating a folder under which you are creating only sublocations, you can choose Locations only. Finally choose OK.

NB! If there is later a need to change the type of location, you can’t unfortunately do it yourself. You will need to contact superusers. You can’t also rename, move or delete a location.

Retiring a container

Locate the container to be retired either from tree structure or by using Search.

Choose the container and choose from gray menu bar Update > Retire Container and click OK. Update > Delete Container should not be used.

Moving a container

Locate the container to be moved either from tree structure or by using Search.

Choose the container and choose from gray menu bar Update > Move Container. From the opening window choose a new location using Browse and click OK.

Editing a container

Locate the container to be edited either from tree structure or by using Search.

Choose the container and choose from gray menu bar Update > Edit Container. Do the desired edits and click OK.

Updating multiple containers at once

Sometimes it is very convenient to update multiple containers at once (for example move or retire containers or to add a certain text to containers, for example Container Owner). You can update up to 99 containers at once. That can be done either using tree structure or Search.

Using tree structure

Go to desired location in tree structure.

Choose from menu View > Multi-Select. You will notice that there is now a checkmark box on the left side of each container. You can individually choose containers or select all containers using Select All. After that you can choose from Update menu the desired action (Move, Retire or Update). If you choose Update, you will be asked which fields you want to update. If you want to update multiple fields, press and hold Ctrl to choose them.

Using Search function

Locate the desired containers using Search (for example by choosing a location in Location ID).

In Search results, please choose Multi-Select and do as instructed in “Using tree structure”. Please notice that Update menu is now on upper part of window.

Creating reports

There is a built-in report tool in inventory but it comes with templates you can’t edit yourself. That’s why it is a much better choice to use Search and you can get only the information you really need ready to be pasted into Excel. Advanced Search is the best option for that (Search > Advanced search). Below are some examples:

Reporting chemicals by hazard class

Go to Search > Advanced search.

Choose the location which you want to report in Location ID.

In Pictograms field you can type the abbreviation of hazard class (GHS01-GHS09, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GHS_hazard_pictograms). For example search string GHS02 gives a list of all flammables.

Search results can be edited using Column Chooser. Here you can add/remove columns. (choose the column you want to add from list on the left, click -> and Save, choose the column you want to remove from the list on the right , click <- and Save). If you want to add/remove multiple columns at once, press and hold Ctrl. Column widths can be changed from Set Widths. When you are happy with the search result view, choose results and copy them to Clipboard. You can now paste them for example into Excel and edit them if needed.

Reporting chemicals by hazard statements

Go to Search > Advanced search.

Choose the location which you want to report in Location ID.

In H statements field you can type the abbreviation of hazard statement (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GHS_hazard_statements). For example H226 gives a list of all chemicals classified as ”Flammable liquid and vapor”. If you want to search by multiple hazard statements, AND and OR can be used (for example H225 OR H226 lists chemicals classified either as H225 or as H226. H301 AND H311 lists chemicals classified both as H301 and as H311).

|

|
